commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f4ee4beac25a57b24699ad69b675905b617fa74e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the old `shouldRethrowAbort` check with an `abortSignal.aborted` guard before failover normalization and adds focused `model-fallback` regression tests.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +51. Total +57 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. The current compaction fallback caller omits `abortSignal`, while its inner compaction timeout wrapper throws `AbortError` on caller cancellation and this PR makes terminal handling depend on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can thread the missing compaction abort signal and add focused regression coverage, though contributor real behavior proof is still required before merge.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

## Fix

Add an explicit guard **before** `coerceToFailoverError` in `runFallbackCandidate`: if the error is an `AbortError` and the external abort signal is already aborted, rethrow immediately (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s (no external signal) fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before coerceToFailoverError normalization:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
  throw err;
}
```

Also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +65 / -8
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard moved before normalization (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: 3 regression tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Provider-side `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` (no external abort signal) was misclassified by `shouldRethrowAbort()` → `isTimeoutError()` matching the message, causing the fallback loop to silently swallow the error. In group/channel sessions this produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`, permanently silencing the topic with zero visible response.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, Vitest 4.1.7 against the production `model-fallback.ts` source (not minified dist). The guard logic and all abort-signal handler paths exercised via the full `model-fallback.test.ts` suite.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:
```
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back on user aborts 53ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back when user cancels with AbortError reason 51ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal) 51ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  79 passed (79)
   Duration  37.49s
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Provider-side AbortError (no signal): guard does NOT fire → error enters `coerceToFailoverError` → falls through to next fallback candidate → topic recovers
  - User cancel (AbortError reason): guard fires (`abortSignal.aborted = true`) → rethrows immediately → no wasted provider calls
  - User cancel (TimeoutError reason): `isTerminalAbort` catches first → rethrows immediately
  - All 79 tests pass, including 3 new regression tests

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway end-to-end with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction (guard logic covered deterministically; full network-layer round-trip not driven). `agent-runner-execution.ts` SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN routing (separate follow-up).
target.author: shengting

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

Add an explicit guard **before** `coerceToFailoverError` in `runFallbackCandidate`: if the error is an `AbortError` and the external abort signal is already aborted, rethrow immediately (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s (no external signal) fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before coerceToFailoverError normalization:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
  throw err;
}
```

Also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +65 / -8
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard moved before normalization (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: 3 regression tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Provider-side `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` (no external abort signal) was misclassified by `shouldRethrowAbort()` → `isTimeoutError()` matching the message, causing the fallback loop to silently swallow the error. In group/channel sessions this produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`, permanently silencing the topic with zero visible response.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, Vitest 4.1.7 against the production `model-fallback.ts` source (not minified dist). The guard logic and all abort-signal handler paths exercised via the full `model-fallback.test.ts` suite.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:
```
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back on user aborts 53ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back when user cancels with AbortError reason 51ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal) 51ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  79 passed (79)
   Duration  37.49s
```

  - Provider-side AbortError (no signal): guard does NOT fire → error enters `coerceToFailoverError` → falls through to next fallback candidate → topic recovers
  - User cancel (AbortError reason): guard fires (`abortSignal.aborted = true`) → rethrows immediately → no wasted provider calls
  - User cancel (TimeoutError reason): `isTerminalAbort` catches first → rethrows immediately
  - All 79 tests pass, including 3 new regression tests

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway end-to-end with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction (guard logic covered deterministically; full network-layer round-trip not driven). `agent-runner-execution.ts` SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN routing (separate follow-up).

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  79 passed (79)
   Duration  37.49s
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Provider-side AbortError (no signal): guard does NOT fire → error enters `coerceToFailoverError` → falls through to next fallback candidate → topic recovers
  - User cancel (AbortError reason): guard fires (`abortSignal.aborted = true`) → rethrows immediately → no wasted provider calls
  - User cancel (TimeoutError reason): `isTerminalAbort` catches first → rethrows immediately
  - All 79 tests pass, including 3 new regression tests

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway end-to-end with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction (guard logic covered deterministically; full network-layer round-trip not driven). `agent-runner-execution.ts` SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN routing (separate follow-up).

commands.author: shengting

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`"externalAbort: false"` in trajectory). The existing guard `shouldRethrowAbort()` returned `false` for these errors (because `isTimeoutError` matched the "operation was aborted" message), so they fell through to the fallback loop but were never retried — the error propagated up and produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN` in group/channel sessions, permanently silencing the topic.

## Fix

Add an explicit guard **before** `coerceToFailoverError` in `runFallbackCandidate`: if the error is an `AbortError` and the external abort signal is already aborted, rethrow immediately (user/gateway cancellation). Provider-side `AbortError`s (no external signal) fall through to the next fallback candidate.

```typescript
// Before coerceToFailoverError normalization:
if (isFallbackAbortError(err) && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
  throw err;
}
```

Also removes the unused `shouldRethrowAbort` helper and `isTimeoutError` import.

## Scope

- **2 files changed**, +65 / -8
- `model-fallback.ts`: guard moved before normalization (core fix)
- `model-fallback.test.ts`: 3 regression tests

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Provider-side `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` (no external abort signal) was misclassified by `shouldRethrowAbort()` → `isTimeoutError()` matching the message, causing the fallback loop to silently swallow the error. In group/channel sessions this produced `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`, permanently silencing the topic with zero visible response.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, Vitest 4.1.7 against the production `model-fallback.ts` source (not minified dist). The guard logic and all abort-signal handler paths exercised via the full `model-fallback.test.ts` suite.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:
```
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back on user aborts 53ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > does not fall back when user cancels with AbortError reason 51ms
 ✓  agents-core  src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts > runWithModelFallback > falls back when AbortError comes from the LLM provider (no external signal) 51ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  79 passed (79)
   Duration  37.49s
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Provider-side AbortError (no signal): guard does NOT fire → error enters `coerceToFailoverError` → falls through to next fallback candidate → topic recovers
  - User cancel (AbortError reason): guard fires (`abortSignal.aborted = true`) → rethrows immediately → no wasted provider calls
  - User cancel (TimeoutError reason): `isTerminalAbort` catches first → rethrows immediately
  - All 79 tests pass, including 3 new regression tests

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway end-to-end with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction (guard logic covered deterministically; full network-layer round-trip not driven). `agent-runner-execution.ts` SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN routing (separate follow-up).
